evals.prompt: ok so we have this customer support chatbot and the responses are kind of hit or miss. I want to find the best prompt setup — maybe test few-shot vs zero-shot vs chain-of-thought across Claude Sonnet and GPT-4o. budget is like $10
evals.expected_output: Creates experiment directory, produces research_brief.md with discovered models and techniques, generates plan.yaml with 3+ templates and 2 models, pauses for user confirmation with cost estimate

assertions.text: Creates experiments/ directory with date-slug naming
research_brief.md contains Discovered LLM Models section with pricing
research_brief.md contains Discovered Prompt Techniques section with at least 4 techniques
plan.yaml exists with axes.templates, axes.parameters, axes.models
plan.yaml budget.max_cost_usd is 10.00
Pauses after PLAN phase to show cost estimate and wait for approval

evals.prompt: I need to optimize content moderation prompts for my e-commerce platform. Test it against as many models as you can find
evals.expected_output: Researches current LLMs via web search, discovers 4-8 models across budget/balanced/premium tiers, creates experiment with content-moderation-specific templates and test data

assertions.text: research_brief.md discovers at least 4 models via web search (not just static references)
research_brief.md identifies task type as classification
Templates include output format for APPROVE/REJECT/ESCALATE or similar classification labels
Test data generated with easy/hard/edge distribution if user didn't provide any
Each model entry has base_url and api_key_env fields

evals.prompt: test gpt-5.4 and glm-5 and llama-4-70b on Together AI for analyzing jira tickets. I have some sample tickets in tickets.jsonl
evals.expected_output: Uses user-specified models, researches their API endpoints and pricing, uses user-provided test data instead of generating

assertions.text: plan.yaml axes.models includes gpt-5.4, glm-5, and llama-4-70b
research_brief.md looks up Together AI base_url and api_key_env for llama-4-70b
plan.yaml data.source is user_provided, not generate
Does not generate test data — uses tickets.jsonl
Models have provider field set (not just name)

evals.prompt: run a quick smoke test on my invoice extraction prompts
evals.expected_output: Triggers fast-test workflow, minimal 2x2 matrix, fast execution

assertions.text: Loads references/workflow-fast-test.md
Uses minimal matrix (2 templates, 2 models or fewer)
Completes faster than full pipeline

evals.prompt: the results from the last experiment weren't great. can you iterate and try to improve the scores? focus on the edge cases
evals.expected_output: Triggers iterative workflow, reads previous evaluations/summary.yaml, narrows parameter ranges around top performers

assertions.text: Loads references/workflow-iterative.md
Reads existing evaluations/summary.yaml from previous experiment
Creates new templates that are variations of previous winners

evals.prompt: I want to find the cheapest model that can still do sentiment analysis with at least 85% accuracy. test haiku, gpt-4o-mini, and gemini flash. budget $3
evals.expected_output: Creates budget-focused experiment with 3 cheap models, includes accuracy as primary evaluation criterion

assertions.text: plan.yaml budget.max_cost_usd is 3.00
All models are budget tier
Evaluation criteria include accuracy with high weight
Report includes cost-performance analysis identifying cheapest model meeting threshold
